name: Docker Scout Container Security API
what_it_does: Container image vulnerability scanning and policy enforcement platform built into Docker — enabling developers and DevSecOps teams to analyze Docker images for OS and package vulnerabilities, view remediation recommendations, generate SBOMs, and enforce security policies via Docker Desktop, Docker Hub, and CLI integration, with a REST API for programmatic access to image analysis results.
use_cases: Scanning Docker images for CVEs via Docker Scout CLI in development workflow
Viewing vulnerability analysis via Docker Scout in Docker Desktop for developer-facing security
Automating image policy enforcement via Docker Scout in CI/CD pipelines
Comparing vulnerability profiles between image tags via Docker Scout quickview
Integrating Docker Scout API with security dashboards for fleet-wide image visibility
not_for: Runtime container security (use Falco or Sysdig for runtime threat detection)
Non-Docker/OCI container runtimes with proprietary formats
Deep SBOM customization and compliance reporting (use Syft/Anchore for advanced SBOM)
best_when: A team already using Docker Hub and Docker Desktop wants integrated container vulnerability scanning without adopting a separate security tool — Scout is built into the Docker ecosystem.
avoid_when: You need advanced policy enforcement, runtime security, or scan non-Docker container images with custom SBOM requirements.

auth.notes: Docker Hub Personal Access Token (PAT) or Docker ID credentials. docker scout CLI authenticates via docker login. REST API uses Bearer token from Docker Hub authentication. Scout API endpoints at api.scout.docker.com. Organization-level access for team features. Free tier limited to 3 repositories.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 3 Docker Hub repositories scanned; Docker Desktop integration free for personal use
pricing.paid_tiers: Docker Team $11/user/month; Docker Business $21/user/month (all with Scout Team features)
pricing.notes: Docker, Inc. (San Francisco). Docker Scout launched 2023, replacing Docker Scan (Snyk-based). Acquired Atomist technology for image analysis. Built on SBOM-first approach. Vulnerability DB: aggregates NVD, OS vendor advisories. Docker Desktop integration: real-time scanning in IDE. docker scout cves for CLI analysis. Policies: no critical CVEs, up-to-date base images. Integration with GitHub Actions, GitLab CI. Docker Hub webhooks for registry events (not Scout-specific). Free tier: 3 repos — very limited for real usage.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; rate limits apply to Docker Hub API

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Docker Hub PAT auth. CLI-first (docker scout). REST API available but limited documentation. Free tier: 3 repos only. SBOM-based scanning. Docker Desktop integration. Launched 2023 — still maturing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Vulnerability reads idempotent by image digest. Scout analysis is deterministic for same image/vulnerability DB version. Policy evaluations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier limited to 3 repositories — paid plan required for real team use
CLI is primary interface: docker scout cves, docker scout quickview, docker scout compare
REST API documentation is sparse — CLI usage is better documented
Image must be pushed to Docker Hub or accessible registry before Scout can analyze it
Scout replaces legacy docker scan (Snyk-based) — different commands and output format
Policy enforcement requires Docker Team/Business plan — not available on free tier

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Docker Hub PAT auth (no OAuth2 granularity). SOC2 certified. SBOM-based vulnerability analysis. Container images may contain application secrets in layers. US data residency. SBOM generation for supply chain transparency. Vulnerability DB aggregates multiple sources (NVD, vendor advisories).
